prompt:
Make the klaus-worker-0 microVM on-demand instead of always-on.

## Context
The microVM starts and stops with every NixOS rebuild/reboot on classic-laddie, adding significant time. The user wants it to be a manual on-demand thing they start/stop when needed.

## Files to modify
- `hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix` lines 48-84: defines `microvm.vms.klaus-worker-0`
- `modules/klaus-worker/default.nix`: the microVM module itself (hypervisor, vcpus, memory, shares, volumes, networking)

## What to do
1. Set `microvm.vms.klaus-worker-0.autostart = false;` (or the equivalent in microvm.nix) so the VM doesn't start automatically on boot or rebuild
2. The systemd service `microvm@klaus-worker-0` should still exist, just not be enabled/wanted by multi-user.target
3. Verify the TAP interface service (`microvm-tap-interfaces@klaus-worker-0`, lines 78-83) still works correctly when the VM is started manually — it depends on `br-klaus-netdev.service`
4. Optionally add a small shell script or note in the commit message about how to manually start/stop: `sudo systemctl start microvm@klaus-worker-0` / `sudo systemctl stop microvm@klaus-worker-0`

## Acceptance criteria
- `microvm@klaus-worker-0` does NOT auto-start on boot
- The VM can still be started manually via systemctl
- Networking (bridge, TAP interface) still works when started manually
- No other services are broken by this change
- Build with `nix build .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel` to verify it compiles
